items.title: Terminal diagnosis hasn't stopped Joan Humble's final exhibition

children.content: After a lifetime of capturing Tasmanian landscapes, Joan Humble is holding her final exhibition. The 88-year-old has a terminal cancer diagnosis, but she is determined to keep painting as long as she can hold a brush.

gallery.altText: Woman sits on chair, using a magnifying glass to paint

thumbnail.altText: Woman sits on chair, using a magnifying glass to paint

items.title: Body found after house fire in north-west Tasmania

children.content: Firefighters believe the body is that of the resident of the Devonport property.

image.altText: A white home with black marks where a fire has charred the roof.

items.title: Man in serious condition after being shot by Tasmania Police

children.content: A man is in a serious but stable condition after being shot by police during an altercation in Hobart's northern suburbs early on Sunday morning.

image.altText: Two police cars parked on a street with a drone in the air

items.title: The cleanest air on Earth holds a powerful lesson about our climate

children.content: Perched on Tasmania's wild north-west coast, Cape Grim has been quietly tracking the world's cleanest air for 50 years. Its data tell an interesting story about how our world has changed over half a century, including rare success stories.

gallery.altText: Graeme Pearman wears a red jacket and red flares, as he sets up a tower at Cape Grim in 1976

thumbnail.altText: Graeme Pearman wears a red jacket and red flares, as he sets up a tower at Cape Grim in 1976

items.title: 'Chock-a-block' cemetery means non-Catholics have no grave options

children.content: The Anglican church in a small northern Tasmanian town has exhausted all its cemetery space after selling off land to accommodate new social and affordable housing.

image.altText: A woman in a blue cardigan stands in a cemetery on a cloudy day.

items.title: More people are using this 'secret deal' to get an EV. Here's what to know

children.content: With the petrol bowser continuing to hurt wallets, more Australians than ever are considering getting an electric vehicle through a novated lease.

image.altText: Image shows a close-up view of a man's hand holding an electric vehicle charger and inserting it in the charging point.

items.title: Price crunch has some servo owners asking: 'Is it really worth doing this?'

children.content: Workers are reporting an increase in abuse from customers as service station owners say they're not making money from fuel price rises.

image.altText: External shots of a 7/11 petrol station and fuel pump with an out of order sign.

items.title: Commuters are switching from cars to e-bikes to save money on fuel

children.content: As high fuel prices continue to affect motorists, Australians are turning to e-bikes as an alternative form of transport, with retailers reporting a surge in sales.

image.altText: Sam smiles in a bike shop.

items.title: Search for 65yo swept away crossing floodwater on foot

children.content: Police are searching for a man in his 60s who was swept away trying to cross a flooded road on foot in SA's far north over the Easter weekend.

image.altText: A man looks into the camera. He has white hair and is wearing a hat.

items.title: Christian villages in Lebanon with no affiliation to Hezbollah attacked by Israel

children.content: Bashir was injured when Israel attacked his home in a Christian village in Lebanon's south, where many residents refuse to leave despite threats of destruction.

image.altText: A large cross and a flag of Lebanon erected on top of hill, underneath large letters read Rmeich

items.title: How one extra tree too close to your house could cost you thousands

children.content: Tree canopy coverage is being boosted in every capital city, but one micro-level analysis has found a link between property prices and the proximity of trees to houses.

gallery.altText: Dark SUV driving down city street with lots of trees in it
A single-storey white house seen at a street level below multiple large trees with bushy green growth towering over it.

thumbnail.altText: Dark SUV driving down city street with lots of trees in it
A single-storey white house seen at a street level below multiple large trees with bushy green growth towering over it.

items.title: Push to axe Kanye West from Wireless festival after past antisemitic remarks

children.content: There is increasing pressure for Ye, formerly known as Kanye West, to be pulled as headliner of the Wireless Festival in London, as UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer weighs in and Pepsi withdraws as lead sponsor.

image.altText: Kanye West performing on stage holding microphone in hand

items.title: A great gift or unexpected curse? Reflections on life as a new retiree

children.content: While time is retirement's great gift, it can become a curse for anyone who has devoted decades to a career and then suddenly wakes up with relevance deprivation syndrome and no firm plan on what comes next, writes Matt Eaton.

image.altText: Three men smile for a selfie. Two hold guitars while another holds up drumsticks while sat at a kit.

items.title: Bic Runga thought her first album in 15 years might never arrive

children.content: "There were definitely times where I thought I was never going to make anything again," the New Zealand star says of new album Red Sunset.

gallery.altText: Bic Runga standing outside an art studio at night, illuminated by colourful window displays.

thumbnail.altText: Bic Runga standing outside an art studio at night, illuminated by colourful window displays.

items.title: Moved into a dirty rental? Here's what you need to know

children.content: Having to clean a new rental property before settling in is a common experience for renters, but landlords have obligations when it comes to rental cleanliness upon moving in.

image.altText: A wide-angle image of a clean and modern apartment with large windows, white kitchen cupboards and wooden floors

items.title: Quiz: How well do you know your history?

children.content: Where was the Venus de Milo found? And what common household item first went on sale in April 1827?

image.altText: photo of Venus de Milo statue

items.title: Angry customers who lost $10m in Zone RV collapse urge ASIC to investigate

children.content: With retirement dreams dashed, customers launch a nationwide campaign for the corporate watchdog to investigate the caravan company's former director over insolvent trading claims.

items.title: Moira Deeming wins Victorian Liberal preselection battle

children.content: The outspoken MP has officially won top spot on the party's upper-house ticket for the Western Metropolitan Region after other candidates withdrew.

image.altText: A woman with shoulder-length black hair, and wearing a purple jacket, smiles.

items.title: Scattered rain, strong wind along Qld coast as forecasters watch TC Maila

children.content: The majority of Queensland will enjoy mild weather this week, with scattered showers across the east coast, while further north, Cyclone Maila is set to intensify.

image.altText: Rainbow over waves with lifesaver holding board

items.title: Vet helping native bat pups has 'never seen mass casualties like this'

children.content: Rescuers are scrambling to save as many flying fox pups as they can, with hundreds of the native bats dead or dying in Top End streets.

gallery.altText: A volunteer feeds a rescued baby flying fox, which is swaddled in a colourful blanket.
A rescued baby bat's wing is examined by a vet.
A rescued baby flying fox wrapped in a blanket.

items.title: Fire-affected towns welcome Easter visitors

children.content: Businesses in fire-affected communities of Victoria welcome holiday-makers and daytrippers, as the recovery continues.

image.altText: A smiling man in a shirt and cap leans on a farm fence

items.title: Cycling great avoids disqualification over train crossing to win Tour of Flanders

children.content: Cycling great Tadej Pogačar wins the one-day classic Tour of Flanders for a record-equalling third time, but only after a controversial train crossing splits the field.

image.altText: Cyclists in the Tour of Flanders wait for a train to pass.

items.title: The 'cheese grater' bowls that fed a generation of skaters

children.content: A set of skate bowls with origins as an outlet for teens in a tough Geelong suburb, once visited by Tony Hawk, may soon be preserved long-term through heritage protection.

gallery.altText: A man wearing a black t-shirt holding a skateboard in front of a graffitied wall.

A grainy picture of a teenager in a red tshirt skating.

items.title: Watchdog seeks upgrade to 'harmful' youth cells to improve access to justice

children.content: South Australia's Youth Court cells do not meet "acceptable minimum standards of safety, dignity and care", resulting in child detainees increasingly appearing in court via video link, the state's youth detention watchdog says.

image.altText: A room with row of small cells with persplex walls on one side, lockers and a TV screen on the other. Two toilets in the far end

items.title: Up to five on the run after smashing car into Brisbane luxury store

children.content: Police say a Mercedes SUV was deliberately driven into the store in the early hours today.

image.altText: Police tape in front of a store with a smashed in door.

items.title: Tim David stars for RCB, Travis Head loses on second last ball

children.content: It is a mixed day for the Australians in the Indian Premier League, with Tim David powering RCB to a victory, while Travis Head fails to make an impact in Sunrisers Hyderabad's loss in Lucknow.

image.altText: Tim David hits a ball towards the camera while playing in the IPL.

items.title: Resurgent Jason Day back at Masters after career of near misses

children.content: After five top-10 finishes at the Masters, including last year, Australian Jason Day returns to Augusta in hot form as he wrestles with his clubs.

image.altText: Golfer Jason Day leans against a tree during a Masters practice round at Augusta National.

items.title: 'Easter bilby' populations booming across sanctuaries

children.content: The Australian Wildlife Conservancy says the threatened species has rebounded at multiple sites across the country.

items.title: Fiji cruise ship runs aground after hitting reef near 'Cast Away island'

children.content: Thirty passengers have reportedly been rescued after a cruise ship ran aground on a reef near the island where the film Cast Away was shot.

image.altText: A three-level cruise ship.

items.title: Feral animals, biosecurity and careers on agenda for young Aboriginal rangers

children.content: The first SA Aboriginal Land and Sea Ranger Conference has brought together those on the front line of the fight against feral animals and plants.

image.altText: A man holding a bag out of which a small warru black footed rock wallaby has jumped out of
